# @zakkster/lite-headless/form-field

Wrapper primitive that wires a label, control, helper text, and error
message into a coherent ARIA pattern. Doesn't own the input value;
that's the consumer's (or a paired primitive's). Owns:

- validity state (valid + errorMessage)
- required flag
- touched flag (true once the control has been blurred; gates error
  display by default)

## API

```js
import { createFormField } from "@zakkster/lite-headless/form-field";

const ff = createFormField({
    defaultValid: true,
    defaultErrorMessage: null,
    defaultRequired: false,
    showErrorsBeforeTouched: false,
    onValidChange: (valid, errorMessage) => {},
    onTouch: () => {},
});
```

## Reactive accessors

```js
ff.valid()         // boolean
ff.errorMessage()  // string | null
ff.required()      // boolean
ff.touched()       // boolean
ff.showsError()    // boolean -- derived; true when invalid AND (touched OR showErrorsBeforeTouched)
```

## Mutations

```js
ff.setValid(true | false, errorMessage)
ff.setRequired(true | false)
ff.setTouched(true | false)
ff.reset()
```

## Attach

```js
ff.attachRoot(el)           // paints data-invalid / data-required / data-touched / data-shows-error
ff.attachLabel(el)          // wires label.for = control.id; paints data-required
ff.attachControl(el)        // wires aria-invalid / aria-required / aria-describedby chain; blur->touched
ff.attachHelperText(el)     // always in aria-describedby
ff.attachErrorText(el)      // role=alert + aria-live=polite; textContent tracks errorMessage
```

Attach order doesn't matter -- the wrapper resolves the wiring when
both pieces are present.

## Painted attributes

### Root (attachRoot)
- `data-invalid`         (when !valid)
- `data-required`        (when required)
- `data-touched`         (when touched)
- `data-shows-error`     (when showsError() is true)

### Label (attachLabel)
- `for="<control.id>"`
- `data-required`

### Control (attachControl)
- `id="lh-ff-control-*"` (auto-assigned)
- `aria-invalid="true|false"`
- `aria-required="true|false"`
- `aria-describedby="<helper.id> <error.id>?"` (error id only when shown)

### Error text (attachErrorText)
- `role="alert"`, `aria-live="polite"`
- `data-hidden` (when !showsError; consumer CSS hides via `[data-hidden]`)

## Custom element

```html
<lite-form-field required>
    <label data-ff-label>Email</label>
    <input data-ff-control type="email">
    <p data-ff-helper>We won't share your email.</p>
    <p data-ff-error></p>
</lite-form-field>
```

### Host API

```js
host.valid             // accessor: boolean
host.errorMessage      // accessor: string | null
host.required          // accessor: boolean
host.touched           // accessor: boolean
host.setValid(v, msg) / .setRequired(r) / .setTouched(t) / .reset()
host._formFieldInstance
```

### Host attributes
- `required`                     -- mirrors setRequired(true)
- `invalid`                       -- mirrors setValid(false)
- `error="<message>"`             -- mirrors setValid(false, "<message>")
- `show-errors-immediately`       -- bypass the touched gate

### Custom element events
- `validchange` `{ detail: { valid, errorMessage } }`
- `touch`

## Composition

Use with any input-bearing primitive:

```js
const ff = createFormField({ defaultRequired: true });
const combobox = createCombobox({ defaultValue: null });
ff.attachControl(triggerEl);
combobox.attachTrigger(triggerEl);

effect(() => {
    const v = combobox.value();
    if (!v) ff.setValid(false, "Selection required");
    else    ff.setValid(true);
});
```

## Pairing with `@zakkster/lite-form` (recommended)

`form-field` handles **ARIA wiring only**: label.for, control.id,
aria-describedby chain, aria-invalid, touched gate. It does NOT own
the input value, run validators, track dirty state, or coordinate
submit. For all of that, pair with [`@zakkster/lite-form`][lf]
&mdash; the canonical state engine in the @zakkster family.

```js
import { createForm }      from "@zakkster/lite-form";
import { createFormField } from "@zakkster/lite-headless/form-field";
import { effect }          from "@zakkster/lite-signal";

const form = createForm({
    initialValues: { email: "" },
    validators: {
        email: (v) => /.+@.+\..+/.test(v) ? null : "Not a valid email",
    },
    validateOn: "blur",
});

const ff = createFormField({ defaultRequired: true });
ff.attachRoot(rootEl);
ff.attachLabel(labelEl);
ff.attachControl(inputEl);
ff.attachHelperText(helperEl);
ff.attachErrorText(errorEl);

// Bridge: form-field reads lite-form's reactive state, lite-form
// owns the value. Each is fine-grained -- typing one field runs
// exactly one validator, then exactly one ARIA paint.
const field = form.field("email");
inputEl.value = field.value();
inputEl.addEventListener("input", (e) => field.set(e.target.value));
inputEl.addEventListener("blur",  () => field.blur());

effect(() => {
    const err = field.error();    // null until "show" gate opens
    ff.setValid(err == null, err);
});
effect(() => { ff.setTouched(field.touched()); });
```

The split exists because the two concerns scale independently:
`lite-form` can drive a 100-field form with ~1.5M keystrokes/sec
benchmarked; `form-field` knows nothing about values and never
re-runs. Validation pipeline lives in `lite-form`; DOM ARIA lives
in `form-field`; consumers wire them together via a few `effect()`
bridges.

[lf]: https://www.npmjs.com/package/@zakkster/lite-form

## Notes

- The blur listener on the control auto-marks the field as touched on
  the first blur. Consumers can also call `setTouched(true)` directly
  (useful for "submit attempted" flows where all fields should be
  marked touched at once).
- Errors are hidden by default until touched -- this is the standard
  UX pattern (don't yell at users before they've interacted). Override
  with `showErrorsBeforeTouched: true` if your form should show errors
  immediately (e.g. server-side validation results).
- The primitive does NOT block submit on its own. Validation logic
  lives in the consumer; the primitive only manages the display state.

## CSS contract

This primitive's painted attributes follow the canonical CSS contract
in `docs/CSS_CONTRACT.md`. See that doc for the cross-primitive
taxonomy (booleans like `data-open` / `data-disabled` / `data-loading`,
enums like `data-status` / `data-side`, ARIA pairings, host accessor
naming, and event naming).
